opportunity.title: What if AI agents could learn from each other's mistakes?
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** The core challenge isn't storing failures but preventing circular reasoning when agents learn from each other's error states. In practice, you need to distinguish between model hallucinations and actual semantic failures—hallucinations can't be "learned" from, only workarounds for. I've found that distributed agent systems work better when they log tool invocations separately from reasoning traces, otherwise you're training agents to mimic bad reasoning patterns.
actions.reasoning: ** I have hands-on experience building multi-agent systems with distributed failure logging and collective learning mechanisms, including work on agent graphs where failures propagate across tool-calling chains.
